---
title: "Responsible Use of VIGIAR Data"
author: "Ryan Santos"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Responsible Use of VIGIAR Data}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

## Source

The package accesses public VIGIAR data made available through Power BI
"Publish to Web" by Brazil's Ministry of Health.

## Main Limitations

### PM2.5

- PM2.5 may be estimated or modelled, not measured by a ground station in every
  municipality.
- Spatial resolution can smooth local hotspots.
- Annual or monthly averages can hide short high-exposure events.

### Population Exposure

- Population counts can be projections rather than direct census counts.
- Aggregate exposure assumes spatial patterns that may not hold within every
  municipality.
- Commuting, occupational exposure, and indoor exposure are not resolved at the
  individual level.

### Health Indicators

- Attributable fractions are model-based epidemiologic estimates.
- Confidence intervals describe model uncertainty, not individual diagnosis.
- Aggregate indicators do not establish individual causality.

## Interpretation Risks

- Do not make causal claims from ecological aggregates alone.
- Do not rank municipalities without checking coverage and uncertainty.
- Do not compare isolated years without inspecting the time series.
- Treat "data available" and "data complete for the research question" as
  different claims.
- Check possible Power BI API truncation before reporting completeness.

## Privacy

The package downloads aggregate public data. It does not collect, store, or
transmit individual-level personal data.

## AI Use Statement

See `AI_USE_DECLARATION.md` for the repository statement about AI-assisted code
and documentation review.
